name: From the Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir to the Galarreta Church — circular from Salvatierra
name_original: Del Embalse Ullíbarri-Gamboa a la Iglesia Galarreta — circular desde Salvatierra
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Salvatierra / Agurain traverse the diverse landscapes of the Llanada Alavesa in the Basque Country. The region is characterized by its position between the Entzia and Iturrieta Mountain Ranges, offering challenging climbs and scenic routes. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from the green landscapes watered by the Zadorra River to demanding mountain passes like Opakua. Ancient greenways also provide flatter options for different cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Salvatierra / Agurain The most popular road cycling route is From the Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir to the Galarreta Church — circular from Salvatierra, a 26.6 miles (42.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Fields of the Llanada Alavesa – Descent to Arbulo loop from Agurain, a moderate 34.3 miles (55.1 km) path. This route winds through the open fields of the Llanada Alavesa. Local road cyclists also love the Aterpe Kampezu Hostel – Opakua Pass loop from Agurain, a 46.1 miles (74.3 km) trail leading through the challenging Opakua Pass, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Road cycling around Salvatierra / Agurain is defined by demanding mountain passes, green river valleys, and ancient greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to difficult ascents. The routes in Salvatierra / Agurain are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 490 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Salvatierra / Agurain's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Salvatierra / Agurain, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the Llanada Alavesa and its surrounding mountain ranges.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from the green landscapes watered by the Zadorra River to challenging mountain passes like Opakua. You'll find routes winding through open fields, alongside reservoirs, and ascending into the Entzia and Iturrieta Mountain Ranges, providing both demanding climbs and scenic, flatter sections.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the Basque Country generally offers pleasant cycling conditions from spring through autumn. Spring brings lush greenery, and autumn provides cooler temperatures and beautiful foliage. Summers are typically warm, ideal for early morning or late afternoon rides.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, there are several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035888646/from-the-ullibarri-gamboa-reservoir-to-the-galarreta-church-circular-from-salvatierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir to the Galarreta Church — circular from Salvatierra&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 26.6-mile (42.9 km) trail offering pleasant views without significant climbs.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its demanding mountain passes. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40324153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aterpe Kampezu Hostel – Opakua Pass loop from Agurain&lt;/a&gt;, which includes the ascent of the Opakua Pass, reaching over 1,000 meters and testing endurance.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the region?
A: Many routes in Salvatierra / Agurain are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6979607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fields of the Llanada Alavesa – Descent to Arbulo loop from Agurain&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 34.3-mile (55.1 km) path through open fields.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: You can encounter diverse natural beauty, including the Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir, the green landscapes of the Zadorra River valley, and the stunning beech woods of the Entzia and Iturrieta Mountain Ranges. The Opakua Pass itself offers panoramic vistas of pastures and forests.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You might pass by sections of the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1630139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco-Navarro Railway Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the medieval walled town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agurain/Salvatierra&lt;/a&gt; itself. Other notable sites in the broader area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4857424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arantzazu Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/837300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy about the routes in Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: The routes in Salvatierra / Agurain are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain passes to scenic river valleys, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists in Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: Salvatierra / Agurain, as a local town, typically offers public parking options. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations and availability, especially in the historic town center, before your ride.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not detailed, the easier, flatter sections, such as parts of the ancient greenways or routes like the one around the Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir, could be suitable for families depending on their experience and the children's ages.

Q: Are the road cycling routes dog-friendly?
A: Road cycling routes themselves are generally open to cyclists. However, if you plan to bring a dog, consider local regulations regarding dogs in public spaces, especially in towns or protected natural areas. Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort, and be mindful of other road users.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay that are cyclist-friendly in Salvatierra / Agurain?
A: Salvatierra / Agurain is a town with a local cycling club, suggesting a welcoming atmosphere for cyclists. You can expect to find various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the town that cater to visitors. It's always a good idea to check with establishments directly for specific cyclist-friendly amenities.
